matched_rules.action: FIND 중 synthetic / fix-insufficient 한 것 abandon, 나머지로 CAND 재편

primary_decision.action: FIND 중 synthetic / fix-insufficient 한 것 abandon, 나머지로 CAND 재편

rationale: Subcause-by-subcause primary-path inversion (CAL-001 style) reveals mixed validity. Subcause 1 (isCrossChannel stale across L161 await) is a REAL race: drainCollectQueueStep is declared async, so even the synchronous `return "skipped"` branch (queue-helpers.ts:167-169) wraps in Promise.resolve and consumes a microtask; enqueueFollowupRun (enqueue.ts:60-109) is fully synchronous to `queue.items.push`, has no lock, and can run in that microtask if a webhook handler's I/O resolves on the same tick. isCrossChannel is computed once at L159 and never re-read, and the batch path at L185-227 iterates `queue.items.slice()` which DOES include the smuggled item. So a single cross-channel item can reach the batch path for one iteration before the next while-iteration re-snapshots. Subcause 2 (auth key excludes channel/to/accountId) is NOT a bug — it is an explicit, documented design decision from PR #66024 (commit 43d4be9027). The comment at drain.ts:62-67 makes it clear: the key is for *per-message authorization / exec-context propagation*, not routing. Display-only and non-authorization fields are intentionally excluded so `profile/name drift does not force conservative splits`. The dedicated test at queue.collect.test.ts:982-1037 locks this behavior in (`senderIsOwner`, `execOverrides`, `bashElevated` change key; display fields do not). Gatekeeper was correct: adding channel here would be a regression against the intended per-message auth semantic and could break documented collect-mode behavior. Subcause 3 (resolveOriginRoutingMetadata per-field `find`) is a REAL latent defect — it picks each of channel/to/accountId/threadId independently, so a mixed-channel groupItems produces chimeric routing (drain.ts:50-60). But it only manifests when groupItems actually contains mixed channels, which requires subcause 1's race. There is no existing fallback/defense — I verified with grep: `resolveOriginRoutingMetadata` has only one call site (drain.ts:209) and no surrounding channel-homogeneity assertion. CAL-001 misses: (a) gatekeeper's 'intended design' concern on subcause 2 is right; a fix that touches auth key would be rejected by maintainers exactly like CAND-004. (b) The upstream fix 712644f0d9 addresses splice extent; PR #66024 added auth split but did NOT add channel homogeneity to resolveOriginRoutingMetadata, nor did it recompute isCrossChannel post-await — these were out of that PR's scope but the boundary between 'auth grouping' and 'channel routing' is deliberately separated. (c) Race window requires same sender bound to multiple channels with near-simultaneous inbound — edge-case frequency. Primary-path inversion: race produces wrong-output only if BOTH (1) cross-channel recompute is absent AND (3) routing picks per-field. Fixing either one blocks the race. Fix at (3) is the right scope: it makes resolveOriginRoutingMetadata pick a single 'dominant' item's routing (e.g., latest item, or assert homogeneity) — small surgical change, no design-intent conflict, no regression risk against PR #66024. Fix at (1) is also viable (recompute isCrossChannel after await) but more intrusive. CAND proposes 'fix any one of three' — proposing subcause 2 is WRONG and would be rejected by maintainers; proposing subcause 1 or 3 is viable. The CAND document conflates the three subcauses as equivalent alternatives without recognizing subcause 2's design-intent wall. Therefore fix-insufficient: the CAND's framing invites an incorrect scope choice.

hidden_guards_found: drain.ts:153-158 comment — 'Once the batch is mixed, never collect again within this drain' — guards *future* iterations via forceIndividualCollect but not the *current* iteration whose isCrossChannel was pre-computed
drain.ts:62-67 comment + PR #66024 — auth key intentionally excludes channel/account; this IS a design guard against over-splitting but also the source of subcause 2 being a non-bug
queue.collect.test.ts:982-1037 — behavioral lock-in test for resolveFollowupAuthorizationKey exclusions; any CAND proposal that adds channel to auth key would have to amend/break these tests
enqueue.ts:70-72 RECENT_QUEUE_MESSAGE_IDS dedupe — rejects replay of same messageId across same routing tuple; does NOT prevent distinct cross-channel enqueue during drain
queue-helpers.ts:167-169 'skipped' fast-path — returns synchronously within an async function, so still yields to microtask queue
role_specific.primary_path_inversion_result: For the symptom (cross-channel combined prompt delivered to wrong channel) to manifest, ALL of the following must hold simultaneously: (A) a cross-channel item is pushed by an external async caller during the microtask window between L159 computation and L185 slice; (B) that item passes dedupe (distinct messageId / routing from existing items); (C) the newly merged batch has auth-equivalent sender (same senderId/exec/bashElevated); (D) resolveOriginRoutingMetadata picks mismatched fields from different items. Guards (A) and (D) are absent; guard (C) is often satisfied (same user); guard (B) is routine. Fix at (A) — recompute isCrossChannel after await — blocks race. Fix at (D) — force single-item or assert-homogeneous routing — also blocks race and has no design-intent collision. Fix at the auth-key dimension (subcause 2 in CAND) is NOT a valid fix point because it contradicts PR #66024's intentional design and is guarded by explicit comment + dedicated test.
cal001_style_misses: CAND treats three subcauses as symmetrically fixable but subcause 2 has explicit design-intent documentation (drain.ts:62-67) plus behavioral lock-in test (queue.collect.test.ts:982-1037) — maintainer will close exactly like CAND-004 ('closing for now, design intent') if a PR touches auth key

CAND does not distinguish 'guard that must exist' from 'guard that exists with opposite polarity': the L153-158 comment is a guard against re-collecting *after* mixed was observed, not against mixing happening mid-iteration. Misreading this boundary echoes the memory-leak-hunter's L250 misclassification in CAL-001

Race repro requires production-equivalent flow: a microtask scheduling that matches real webhook handler timing (not just vi.useFakeTimers advance). Per CAL-001 section C, any SOL draft must include production-equivalent timing proof (not just synthetic Promise.resolve interleave)

The 'three subcauses, fix any one' framing encourages the least-invasive SOL to chase subcause 2 (auth key) because it looks smallest — but that path is the one most likely to be rejected. Critical-devil call: SOL must NOT target auth key.

rationale: collect-mode drain batch fallthrough (drain.ts:185-227) is on the default drain path — every collect-mode drain iteration with isCrossChannel=false and forceIndividualCollect=false enters it unconditionally after the microtask yield at L161. However the specific cross-channel mixed-batch mis-routing that FIND-002 describes requires (1) queueKey collision across channels, (2) collect mode, (3) same authorization key, and (4) concurrent inbound within the microtask window. Queue key is built via buildAgentSessionKey (resolve-route.ts:93) which embeds channel for group/channel peers and for dmScope=per-peer/per-channel-peer/per-account-channel-peer. The onboarding default dmScope is `per-channel-peer` (onboard-config.ts:5), so out-of-the-box the queueKey differs per channel and cross-channel items cannot collide in one queue. The cross-channel batch collision is only reachable when `session.dmScope="main"` (session-key.ts:141-171 returns `agent:<id>:main` with no channel for direct peers in main scope) AND both channels route to the same agent AND collect mode is enabled AND same-sender DMs land within the ~ microtask window after debounce. That makes the race **configuration-gated edge case** rather than a typical production path. The fallthrough path itself IS hot (score 4/5 for collect-mode collect-channel deployments) but the specific wrong-output manifestation is edge-case (1-2/5). The three subcauses are real code smells even outside the race (resolveOriginRoutingMetadata chimera can also occur if two items legitimately land in one queue with partial metadata), so a scoped fix on (c) routing-identity or (a) isCrossChannel-recompute is worth pursuing at reduced severity — P3 rather than P2.

callers_of_target: scheduleFollowupDrain (drain.ts:135) — called by agent-runner-helpers.ts:64 as the primary drain entrypoint for every followup run; also self-restarts at drain.ts:269 when new items arrive post-finally
kickFollowupDrainIfIdle (drain.ts:37) — called by enqueue.ts:107 whenever enqueueFollowupRun lands with queue.draining=false
enqueueFollowupRun (enqueue.ts:60) — called by agent-runner.ts:1002 (enqueue-followup action) and indirectly by webhook/inbound handlers that instantiate followup runs
drainCollectQueueStep (queue-helpers.ts:177) — only caller for the followup queue path is drain.ts:161; subagent-announce-queue.ts:135 is a separate consumer
hasCrossChannelItems (queue-helpers.ts:233) — only invoked at drain.ts:159 for the followup flow (subagent-announce-queue.ts:110 separate)

rationale: 세 subcause 모두 소스 코드에서 재확인됨. (a) drain.ts:159 의 isCrossChannel 은 L161 await 이전 1회 계산, await 이후 재계산 경로 부재. grep 으로 hasCrossChannelItems 호출이 해당 파일에서 L159 단 1회임을 검증. (b) drain.ts:68-81 resolveFollowupAuthorizationKey 의 키 구성 (senderId/E164/isOwner/execOverrides/bashElevated) 에 channel/to/accountId 가 전혀 포함되지 않음. 주석도 '세션-레벨 or 인가 미검사 필드는 고의로 제외' 라고 명시 — 채널을 고려하지 않은 것이 의도 아님. (c) drain.ts:50-60 resolveOriginRoutingMetadata 의 네 필드 (originatingChannel/To/AccountId/ThreadId) 는 각각 독립 items.find() 로 뽑혀 chimera routing 가능. 주석 'Support both number (Telegram topic) and string (Slack thread_ts)' 가 오히려 cross-channel thread ID 혼합 가능성을 암묵적으로 인정. drainCollectQueueStep (queue-helpers.ts:177-192) 은 async function 이라 skipped path 에서도 Promise 반환으로 microtask yield 유발 검증됨. enqueue.ts:96 queue.items.push 가 draining 체크 없이 실행되므로 window 는 실재. upstream 712644f0d9 은 splice(0)→splice(0, N) 수정만으로 3-file minimal patch (git show 확인), 본 race 는 커버 안함. confidence medium 인 이유: race window 가 microtask 1 tick 으로 좁고, 동일 sender 가 cross-channel bound 된 환경이 실사용에서 얼마나 흔한지 정량 데이터 없음.

production_impact_scenarios.scenario: Multi-channel same-owner concurrent inbound — Telegram 메시지 2개 debounce 중 Slack webhook 이 L161 await microtask 틈에 enqueue. L185 snapshot 에 Slack 포함되나 isCrossChannel 은 stale(false). 결과: Slack 메시지 내용이 Telegram chat 으로 배달되고 Slack 유저는 응답 받지 못함 (silent message loss).

Thread ID chimera — resolveOriginRoutingMetadata 의 독립 find 로 item[0] 의 channel=telegram 과 item[1] 의 threadId=slack_thread_ts 가 조합된 invalid routing 생성. 잘못된 thread 에 응답 전달 또는 채널 adapter 에서 runtime error 가능.

Webhook fan-in 고빈도 production — whatsapp group/discord server 등 collect mode 활성 환경에서 debounce 만료 직후 drain 진입과 외부 webhook 동시성 fan-in 시 race window 반복 적중. microtask 1 tick 에 한정되나 observable rate 는 non-zero.

role_specific.maintainer_receptiveness: HIGH — 메인테이너 가이드 'bugs, critical issues, reliability and stability' 에 정확히 부합. feature 가 아닌 concurrency race / reliability 이슈이며 wrong-output (silent message loss) 은 신뢰성 관점에서 P2 적정. upstream 이 바로 직전 커밋 712644f0d9/43d4be9027 에서 같은 파일 (drain.ts) 의 queue 관련 버그 2건을 연속으로 fix 한 전례 존재 — 이 경로에 대한 maintainer attention 이 활성. CODEOWNERS 제약 파일 (cron/service/jobs.ts, agents/*auth*, agents/sandbox*) 에 해당하지 않아 승인 문턱 낮음. 해결 scope 가 단일 파일 국소 수정으로 XS 규모 가능 (one thing per PR 충족).
role_specific.reproducibility: MEDIUM-HIGH — JS async 명세상 async function 의 반환은 항상 microtask yield 를 유발하므로 race window 존재는 deterministic. 재현 테스트는 vi.useFakeTimers + 수동 enqueueFollowupRun 으로 microtask 틈 injection 가능. reply-flow.test.ts 의 기존 harness 확장 가능. 단 production 실측 빈도 증거는 없으므로 regression test 로 input 유발 방식 검증 필요. forceIndividualCollect=true 이후 iteration 은 guard 되므로 첫 iteration 만 공격 표면.
concerns_acknowledged: race window 가 microtask 1 tick 으로 매우 좁아 실제 production hit rate 불명
동일 sender 가 cross-channel account 에 동시 bound 된 설정이 얼마나 흔한지 미측정 — openclaw 일반 사용자 분포에 따라 edge case 가능
resolveFollowupAuthorizationKey 에서 channel 을 의도적으로 제외한 설계의 원래 의도 (동일 user cross-channel dedupe 의도 여부) 확인 필요 — 의도된 설계라면 auth key 수정이 회귀 유발 가능하며 resolveOriginRoutingMetadata 항등성 강제만으로 축소 fix 권장
severity P2 는 잠정치이며 bug report 데이터 부재 상태에서 P3 로 격하될 가능성
기존 테스트 (reply-flow.test.ts, e2e) 가 이 interleaving 을 cover 하지 않는 점이 확인됨 — 재현 테스트 신규 작성 필요

rationale: upstream/main HEAD (8879ed153d) 의 src/auto-reply/reply/queue/drain.ts 최신 코드에서 CAND-013 의 세 subcause (a) isCrossChannel stale await race, (b) resolveFollowupAuthorizationKey channel 누락, (c) resolveOriginRoutingMetadata 필드 독립 find 키메라 — 모두 그대로 존재함. 6주 내 관련 commit 7개 검토: 712644f0d9 는 splice 범위 수정만, 43d4be9027 (#66024) 는 auth context 로 split 을 도입했으나 auth key 자체에 channel 을 포함시키지 않아 subcause (b) 미해결 — 오히려 CAND-013 이 참조하는 동일 splitCollectItemsByAuthorization 함수를 도입한 commit. 다른 commit (02e07a157d, 3de42e946a, a35dcf608e, 4ca84acf24) 은 callback/concurrency 경로로 본 race 와 무관. 관련 open PR #52423 (maximveksler) 는 subcause (c) 만 단일 source item 선택으로 수정하는 좁은 fix 지만 2026-03-22 이후 open 상태, merge 전. PR #20490 (Jackten) 은 subcause (b) 에 해당하는 routing tuple partitioning 을 제안했으나 CLOSED not-merged. 따라서 upstream duplicate 아님, 단 subcause (c) 는 open PR 과 겹침 — SOL 단계에서 #52423 과의 중복 회피 필요.

recent_related_commits.note: splice(0) -> splice(0, groupItems.length) 수정만. race 미해결.

recent_related_commits.note: splitCollectItemsByAuthorization 도입 commit 본체. auth key 에 channel 미포함이 설계 그대로, subcause (b) 미해결.

recent_related_commits.subject: fix(reply): clear idle followup callbacks
recent_related_commits.note: callback lifecycle, 본 race 와 무관.

recent_related_commits.subject: fix(reply): preserve no-debounce inbound concurrency
recent_related_commits.note: no-debounce 경로. collect batch 경로와 별개.

related_open_prs.title: fix(routing): prevent cross-channel reply routing in same-provider multi-channel setups
related_open_prs.note: subcause (c) 만 narrow fix. (a), (b) 미커버. 한 달째 open — merge 지연 risk.

related_open_prs.title: fix(queue): partition followup routing safety
related_open_prs.overlaps_subcause: b (queue key 에 routing tuple fingerprint)
related_open_prs.note: 2026-02 에 CLOSED not-merged. 유사 접근이 이미 rejected 이력.

role_specific.cal004_risk_assessment: medium — upstream PR #52423 가 subcause (c) 만 건드리는 좁은 형태로 OPEN. CAND-013 SOL 이 (c) 축만 고치는 방향으로 가면 #52423 과 완전 중복 (CAL-004 시나리오 재현). CAND-013 의 세 subcause 중 (a) isCrossChannel 재계산 또는 (b) auth key 에 channel/to/accountId 포함 중 하나를 주 fix 로 선택하면 upstream 대비 novel. (c) 축 단독 선택은 피해야 함. 또한 PR #20490 이 (b) 방향으로 이미 CLOSED 된 이력은 maintainer 가 해당 접근에 보수적일 수 있음을 시사 — SOL 작성 시 testable race 시나리오 재현 테스트 포함 필수.

rationale: 세 subcause 는 production code 에 실재한다. (a) drain.ts:159 isCrossChannel 은 L161 await 이전 snapshot, 재계산 경로 없음. (b) resolveFollowupAuthorizationKey(L68-81) 는 channel/to/accountId 를 의도적으로 (주석 L62-67) 제외. (c) resolveOriginRoutingMetadata(L50-60) 네 필드가 독립 find. drainCollectQueueStep 의 skipped path 도 async function 이므로 microtask yield 실재 (JS spec). enqueueFollowupRun(enqueue.ts:96) 는 queue.draining=true 여도 queue.items.push 를 unconditional 수행 — 외부 webhook 이 drain 중 item 추가 가능. CAL-003 의 hot-path-vs-test-path 불일치와는 다른 종류: branch mismatch 는 없음. 다만 3 subcause 가 동시 만족하는 production trigger 는 (i) 동일 sender 가 여러 채널에 bound + (ii) collect mode 활성 + (iii) 서로 다른 채널의 메시지가 precisely await-boundary microtask 에 도착, 라는 edge case triple. 또한 subcause (c) 의 chimera routing 은 race 없이도 cross-channel mixed batch 가 splitCollectItemsByAuthorization 으로 한 그룹이 되는 모든 경로에서 재현 가능한 pure-function bug 이므로 race 에 의존하지 않고도 테스트 가능. 따라서 '3 subcause 결합 race' 로 프레이밍하면 synthetic 에 가까워지지만, 개별 subcause (특히 c, 그리고 a 의 stale snapshot + mixed-group 분기 미인식) 는 unit-level 로 deterministic 재현 가능. SOL 은 race interleaving 증명에 집중하기보다 (c) 의 routing 항등성 또는 (b) 의 auth key 에 channel 포함 하나만 guard 로 세우면 silent message loss 벡터는 차단되므로 fix 방향은 narrow 하게 scope down 이 바람직.

role_specific.production_trigger: 동일 owner 가 Telegram 과 Slack 에 동시 bound 된 setup + collect mode (group chat/batch reply 원하는 채널) + debounce(1s) 만료 직후 drain 진입 microtask 창에 다른 채널 webhook 이 enqueueFollowupRun 호출. Node single-threaded 이지만 webhook handler 는 async 로 event loop 에 task 를 넣으므로 L161 await yield 구간에 reentrancy 가능. 단 '같은 owner 가 여러 채널 + collect mode + precisely-timed concurrent inbound' 는 edge case triple 이며 고빈도 production 이 아님.
role_specific.cal003_parallel: differs — CAL-003 는 production 이 해당 branch 를 타지 않는 code-path mismatch. CAND-013 은 code path 자체는 reachable (await yield unconditional, webhook enqueue 경로 실재) 이고 문제는 interleaving 확률의 낮음. 단 subcause (c) chimera routing 은 race 와 무관하게 mixed-group 경로에서 재현되는 pure bug 라는 점에서 CAL-003 과 본질적으로 다름.
